summary: Checked site status, lighthouse scores, Search Console, AdSense, and traffic data, plus the latest GitHub Actions deploy and the awesome-devtools backlink PR (#401, still open/unmerged, no action needed there since it lives in a different repo). Site returns 200 with zero broken pages and the most recent deploy succeeded. Lighthouse performance bounced around (73/99/92/84 across recent snapshots) which is normal CI run-to-run noise, not a real trend, and there's no accessibility/best-practices history yet to judge a trend from. Search Console is still empty (expected for a low-traffic new site) and AdSense remains NEEDS_ATTENTION with null earnings (expected, pending Google approval). Traffic is low and steady (~34 pageloads today) spread thinly across all existing tools, not concentrated enough to justify adding a new tool yet, so no code change was made today.

Ran the daily check: site returns 200 with zero broken pages, and the latest deploy (run #23, matching the current main commit) completed successfully. Lighthouse performance sits at 84 today, well within the normal 73-99 run-to-run bounce seen in recent history, so no real quality regression. Search Console is still empty and AdSense is still NEEDS_ATTENTION with null earnings, both expected while the site is new and pending Google's ad approval. Traffic remains thin (34 pageloads today) spread across all nine existing tools with no single page standing out, and the awesome-devtools backlink PR #401 is still open and unmerged with no new activity, so there was nothing actionable to fix and no data-driven signal strong enough to justify a new tool or content change today.

This is a repeat run within the same day (the underlying status snapshot hasn't refreshed since the earlier checks a few minutes ago), so the picture is unchanged: the site responds 200 with no broken pages, the latest GitHub Actions deploy for the current main commit finished successfully, and lighthouse performance (84) is well inside the run-to-run noise band already seen today. Search Console is still empty and AdSense is still NEEDS_ATTENTION with null earnings, both expected for a brand-new, pre-approval site. Traffic stayed thin (34 pageloads) with no page pulling ahead of the others, and the awesome-devtools backlink PR #401 remains open with no new activity, so there's no fresh signal to act on and no code change was made.

Checked the daily snapshot (still dated 2026-07-17 14:09 UTC, about 17 hours old and within the freshness window) and found the site healthy: 200 status, zero broken pages, and the latest deploy to main (commit a7982f3) completed successfully. Lighthouse performance is 84, within the noisy 73-99 range seen across the last few same-day runs, so no real regression; accessibility (88), best-practices (78), and SEO (100) have no multi-day history yet to judge a trend from. Also found and fixed a real housekeeping issue: this checkout's local main branch had drifted from a stale cached view of origin, so I re-synced and confirmed origin/main and the deploy history now match (no content was changed, just branch bookkeeping). Search Console remains empty and AdSense remains NEEDS_ATTENTION with null earnings, both still expected pre-approval. Traffic stayed thin (34 pageloads) and spread across all nine tools with nothing standing out, and the awesome-devtools backlink PR #401 is still open and unmerged with no new activity, so there was no data-driven signal to justify a code, content, or tool change today.

Checked site status, deploy health, and the awesome-devtools backlink PR. Site is returning 200 with zero broken pages and the latest Cloudflare Pages deploy succeeded. Lighthouse performance keeps swinging wildly day to day (57 to 99 over the past week) with no consistent direction, so treating it as CI noise rather than a real regression. Search Console is still empty and AdSense is still NEEDS_ATTENTION, both expected for a low-traffic new site; traffic is trending up slightly (30 to 42 weekly pageloads). PR #401 on awesome-devtools is still open and unmerged, just has an automated bot comment restating submission criteria that Bracketly already satisfies. Nothing warranted a code change today.

Big update today: AdSense account_state flipped from NEEDS_ATTENTION to READY for the first time (it changed sometime on 2026-07-19, after yesterday's run had already read the snapshot, so this is the first check to actually see it). Earnings are still null since no ads have served yet. Digging into why: the AdSense verification script (correct publisher ID ca-pub-5868369279571926) and ads.txt are already live and correct, but the actual per-page ad placement in src/components/AdUnit.astro is still the shipped placeholder with its <ins> block commented out, so no real ad unit is being rendered on any page yet. Finishing activation needs an ad-slot ID from the AdSense dashboard (or confirming Auto ads is toggled on there), which isn't available to this session, so I didn't guess a fake slot ID and made no code change today rather than risk a broken or non-functional ad unit. Everything else is healthy: site returns 200 with zero broken pages, the latest deploy succeeded, lighthouse performance (99) is within normal day-to-day noise, Search Console is still empty as expected, weekly traffic keeps climbing (30 to 65 pageloads over the past week), and the awesome-devtools backlink PR #401 remains open and unmerged with no new activity.
